description_long: This triumphal arch features a double-arched opening framed by a semi-circular arch above, constructed from reddish-brown brickwork with a contrasting light gray stone or concrete trim. The front face displays two symmetrical archways supported by a single central column with a simple capital, standing on a rectangular base. The brickwork is clearly defined, with the arch's keystone and voussoirs rendered in the same light gray as the trim. The rear of the structure mirrors the front but lacks the central column detail, revealing the open passage through both arches. The sides are flat and show the thickness of the brick wall, emphasizing the robust construction. The overall design is reminiscent of Romanesque or early Roman triumphal arches, with minimal decorative reliefs and a focus on structural form.
description: A double-arched brick triumphal arch with a central column, light gray trim, and a semi-circular top, evoking Romanesque architectural style. The structure is robust, with open passageways and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing two archways, a central column, and detailed brickwork with light gray trim.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the brick wall and the semi-circular arch profile.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the open passage through both arches and the absence of the central column detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the wall's depth and arch curvature.
